writeup: The Buffalo Bills wrapped up their 2026 mandatory minicamp on June 11, capping a busy three-day session with five roster moves, including the waiving of cornerback Dorian Strong with an injury designation. Outside linebacker Bradley Chubb emerged as a notable storyline, expressing motivation to win games and play physical while building chemistry with pass-rush partner TJ Parker. The team also received concerning injury updates on safety Cole Bishop and linebacker Dorian Williams, both working through recovery. Wide receiver Deven Thompkins added another layer to the roster news by signing with Buffalo amid the flurry of moves. As the offseason program concludes, fans and analysts are already grading the Bills' 2026 offseason work ahead of training camp.
